title: stone-bowl-dolsot spicy tunisian oysters bowl
description: a citrusy, molten, lacto-vegetarian-friendly tunisian dish featuring oysters served in a stone-bowl-dolsot with a bamboo-tray vessel
instructions: fill the stone-bowl-dolsot with water and bring to a boil.
immediately add the kamut couscous, cover, and let steam for 10 minutes.
while the couscous cooks, mix the lemon juice, olive oil, white peppercorns, garam masala, chili flakes, and salt in a small bowl.
add the oysters to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
add the zest of 1 lemon and toss to combine.
add the cooked kamut couscous to the bowl and toss to combine with the oysters and marinade.
transfer the stone-bowl-dolsot to the preheated oven and cook for 5 minutes, or until the couscous is lightly browned and crispy around the edges.
remove the stone-bowl-dolsot from the oven and sprinkle with parsley.
add the harissa on top and serve immediately.
serve the dish in the bamboo-tray, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
serve the dish immediately in the stone-bowl-dolsot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
